title: phyllanthus subcuneatus
intro: Phyllanthus subcuneatus, also known as the fan-leaved phyllanthus, is a species of plant in the family Phyllanthaceae that is native to tropical Africa. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the ability to inhibit the replication of viruses. The health benefits of Phyllanthus subcuneatus include the treatment of fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
